# TUIcast

> Cross-platform CLI that records any terminal app and produces an animated GIF.

TUIcast spawns a target binary in a PTY, injects scripted keystrokes, captures
all terminal output as an asciinema v2 cast, then renders an animated GIF via
agg. No manual screen recording, no browser-based tools.

## Install

Download a release archive from https://github.com/gui-cs/TUIcast/releases
(includes tuicast + agg). Or: `go install github.com/gui-cs/TUIcast/cmd/tuicast@latest`
and install agg separately (https://github.com/asciinema/agg).

## Quick start

```sh
tuicast record \
    --binary ./my-app \
    --keystrokes "wait:2000,Hello world,wait:1500,Esc" \
    --output demo.gif
```

## AI Agent usage

Run `tuicast agent-guide` to get the full keystroke syntax reference,
best practices, and examples for AI-driven recordings.

The guide covers:
- Keystroke script syntax (wait:N, named keys, modifiers, literal text, mouse)
- Terminal.Gui app defaults (--kitty-keyboard, --startup-delay, etc.)
- Known gotchas (cursor/page/arrow literal splitting, --agg-path requirement)
- Composing demo recordings step by step

## Key flags

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| --binary | Path to executable to record |
| --keystrokes | Comma-separated script (wait:N, key names, literal text) |
| --output | Output GIF path |
| --cast-output | Also save raw .cast file |
| --show-command | Synthetic shell prompt pre-roll |
| --startup-delay | Ms to wait before capturing output |
| --kitty-keyboard | Enable Kitty protocol (disambiguates Ctrl+M/Enter etc.) |
| --agg-path | Path to agg binary (if not on PATH) |
| --verbosity | quiet, normal, or high |
| --cols / --rows | Terminal dimensions |
| --line-height | Line-height multiplier (default 1.3) |
| --font | Font family for GIF rendering |

## Links

- Repository: https://github.com/gui-cs/TUIcast
- Releases: https://github.com/gui-cs/TUIcast/releases
- Agent guide (in-repo): agent/RECORDING-AGENT.md
- Spec: spec.md
